title: Rhino Scene-Graph Artifact
description: Externalized world model owned by the rhino-scene-state skill. It is the canonical, re-read-every-step record of what actually exists in the live Rhino document: a part_id -> GUID ledger plus a bbox/dimension ledger, and the realized spatial relations between parts. It is the realized counterpart to the build-plan IR (shared/build-plan.schema.json): the IR declares intent, the scene-graph records the baked truth captured at bake time. Producers append GUIDs out-of-band here; the reconcile loop diffs this artifact's nodes against a live document summary to flag MISSING / EXTRA / MIS-SIZED parts and post-boolean count/volume mismatches (corrections C1 and C2).

object.description: Plain-language name of the modeled object, copied from the build-plan IR 'object' field for provenance and verify questions.

units.description: Document unit system, copied from the IR. Every bbox/dims/origin number in this artifact is in these units. Must match sc.doc.ModelUnitSystem at bake time.

tolerance.description: Absolute model tolerance in 'units', copied from the IR / read live off sc.doc.ModelAbsoluteTolerance. Used as the default bbox/dimension match tolerance during reconcile unless overridden per-node.

world_frame.description: Named base construction plane all node local frames are relative to. Default WorldXY.

revision.description: Monotonically increasing artifact revision, bumped after every mutation cycle so re-reads can detect staleness.

last_op.description: Short label of the most recent mutation that produced this revision, e.g. 'boolean_union:seat+legs' or 'bake:leg[2]'. Aids the reconcile report and debugging.

nodes.description: Realized parts. Each node mirrors one build-plan IR part (by part_id) and records its baked GUID, world-axis-aligned bounding box, and key dimensions captured from the live document.

edges.description: Realized spatial/logical relations between nodes, mirroring the IR relation set. Each edge names a relation type and its two endpoint part_ids.

checkpoints.description: Per-stage build checkpoints (conventions §12). One entry per stage that has been baked + reconciled, recording its pass/fail status and the revision at which it last passed. Lets a staged build resume after a crash, lets a re-emit of one stage skip already-passing stages, and (with the .3dm Save the modeling skill triggers at each checkpoint) is the persisted ledger that survives a rollback (E11).

stage.description: Stage id, matching node.stage and the IR stages[].id.

status.description: passed = this stage baked and reconciled clean; failed = reconcile flagged a defect not yet repaired; pending = declared but not yet built.

revision.description: scene-graph revision at which this stage last reached 'status'. The .3dm was saved at this revision (E11).

part_ids.description: Part ids this checkpoint covers (the stage's parts), so a re-emit knows exactly what to purge + rebuild.

connectivity.description: Realized connectivity results for the edges in this stage's scope (§13/C9). Produced by the per-stage sweep (B1) which evaluates only relations whose endpoints are in current/closed stages (B3). A checkpoint with ANY entry of status 'out_of_band' or 'uncovered' for a non-floating edge MUST NOT be marked status='passed' — that is the clause defeating false confidence. Per B1 the sweep returns only violations; passing entries are optional here. Per C2 a pre-purge referential check adds an 'uncovered' entry for every edge pointing at a now-deleted part_id rather than silently passing by omission.

connectivity_status.description: Roll-up of this stage's connectivity sweep: green = every in-scope non-floating edge measured and in-band; violations = at least one out_of_band or uncovered entry => the stage is NOT done; not_run = the sweep has not executed for this revision (e.g. a re-emit of a crossing stage invalidated it per §13/C1 and it must re-run before the stage is green again).

point3d.description: [x, y, z] in document 'units' against world_frame.

bbox.description: World-axis-aligned bounding box captured from the baked geometry via Rhino.Geometry.BoundingBox (GetBoundingBox(True)). min and max are opposite corners in 'units'. UNSOUND as a proximity handle for helical/rotated parts: two far-apart helix points can have touching AABBs, so connectivity (§13/A2) must use 'obb'/'centroid' and realized solid-to-solid measurement, never AABB arithmetic, for non-axis-aligned relations.

obb.description: Oriented bounding box captured at bake time (the A2 oriented handle). Unlike the world-AABB this travels with the part's own frame, so it is a SOUND proximity handle for helical/rotated/curved members where the AABB is misleading. Captured via Rhino.Geometry.Brep.GetBoundingBox(plane) on the part's construction Plane (origin + x_axis + y_axis define the box plane; z_axis is x_axis cross y_axis). The connectivity sweep (§13) uses this and 'centroid' for orientation/coarse culling, then the realized solid-to-solid measurement (A1/A4) for the actual gap.

origin.description: Box plane origin [x,y,z] in 'units' against world_frame.

x_axis.description: Unit X axis of the box plane.

y_axis.description: Unit Y axis of the box plane (z_axis = x_axis x y_axis).

extents.description: Half-or-full extents [ex,ey,ez] along the box plane axes, in 'units' (full edge lengths; the executor that writes this records which convention it used consistently).

frame.description: The local construction frame the part was authored on, copied from the IR part.frame. Lets a re-read reconstruct where the part lives without re-querying geometry.

plane.description: Named target plane (WorldXY, WorldYZ, WorldZX/WorldXZ, or a custom frame name) the part was relocated onto.

x_axis.description: Optional explicit plane X axis vector.

y_axis.description: Optional explicit plane Y axis vector.

node.description: One realized part. MUST carry at least one stable handle: a 'guid' (the canonical Rhino reference) OR a 'part_id' (the UserString fallback resolver, correction C1). In steady state both are present; immediately after a mutator that has not yet returned its GUID, part_id alone is permitted and the reconcile loop backfills the guid.

part_id.description: Stable part identifier, identical to the IR part.id and to the baked object's UserString 'part_id'. The fallback resolver when a GUID is lost (e.g. after a boolean consumes inputs).

guid.description: The Rhino System.Guid returned by AddBrep/AddCurve/AddSurface at bake time, as a string. The canonical object handle (correction C1). May be the unset GUID string only transiently before backfill.

name.description: Rhino object Name (human-readable in the object table), set equal to part_id at bake time.

layer.description: Target Rhino layer name the object lives on, mirroring the IR part.layer.

stage.description: Build stage that baked this node, mirroring the IR part.stage / stages[].id and the baked object's UserString 'stage' (conventions §12). Lets reconcile run SCOPED to a single stage (--stage) so re-emitting one stage is checked against only that stage's expected nodes, not the whole model.

primitive.description: How the geometry was produced: a primitive type ('box','cylinder','sphere','cone','plane','interpolated_curve') or an operation result ('loft','sweep1','revolve','extrude','shell','boolean'). Free-form to allow operation results.

frame.description: Local construction frame the part was authored on (copied from the IR).

bbox.description: World-axis-aligned bounding box captured from the live baked geometry. NOT a proximity handle for non-axis-aligned parts; see 'obb' (A2).

obb.description: Oriented bounding box captured at bake time (A2). REQUIRED-IN-PRACTICE for any helical/rotated/curved member so the connectivity sweep (§13) has a sound oriented handle and never falls back to AABB arithmetic for that part's relations.

centroid.description: Realized solid/area centroid [x,y,z] in 'units' (VolumeMassProperties.Compute(...).Centroid, or AreaMassProperties for open geometry), captured at bake time (A2). A stable single-point handle for orientation/coarse culling in the connectivity sweep; the actual gap is still measured solid-to-solid (A1).

contact_point.description: Optional realized attach point [x,y,z] in 'units' captured at bake time for a curved/helical support (A2/A4): the point on THIS part's realized solid nearest the support it lands on/meets, so a re-emit can re-key the same contact. Informational; the §13 sweep still re-measures live solid-to-solid by GUID (A1) rather than trusting this stored point.

dims.description: Key realized dimensions in 'units', e.g. {x,y,z} for a box, {radius,height} for a cylinder, or {width,depth,height} derived from the bbox. Used by reconcile MIS-SIZED checks.

volume.description: Realized solid volume in 'units^3' from Rhino.Geometry.VolumeMassProperties.Compute, when the node is a closed solid. Used by the post-boolean total-volume check (correction C2). Omitted for open curves/surfaces.

is_solid.description: Whether the baked Brep is a closed solid (Brep.IsSolid). Open curves/surfaces are false.

expected_solid_count.description: For boolean/operation results: how many disjoint solids the result SHOULD contain per the IR boolean_plan. The reconcile loop compares this to the realized count to catch partial boolean failure (correction C2).

provenance.description: Why this part exists / where its shape came from, copied from the IR part.provenance.

color.description: Optional vision color assigned for a colored-part capture (correction C4), e.g. 'red' or '255,0,0'. A render concern, not an identity concern.

edge.description: A realized relation between two nodes, mirroring an IR relation. Edges are keyed by 'from'/'to' part_id ONLY, NEVER by GUID (C1): a re-bake gives a part a new GUID but the same part_id, so part_id-keyed edges survive a re-emit and let the cross-stage invalidation (§13/C1) find every edge crossing into a re-emitted stage. For 'interpenetrate' edges (union joins) the realized overlap depth is recorded in 'penetration' for the correction-C3 check. The measured gap and its pass/out_of_band/uncovered status live in the per-checkpoint 'connectivity' section, not on the edge.

type.description: Relation kind, mirroring the IR relation enum. 'child_of' models an operation result owning its consumed inputs; 'interpenetrate' models a union join overlap; lands_on/meets/spans/spans_between are measured contact obligations (§13/A3).

from.description: part_id of the node this relation is anchored on (NEVER a GUID, C1). For an array family member, the instance id '<id>#<i>'.

to.description: part_id of the other node (NEVER a GUID, C1), or a symmetry/frame name for 'symmetric_about'. For 'spans_between' this is the first support; the second is 'to2'.

to2.description: Second support part_id for type='spans_between' (the part is supported at both endpoints; both gaps are measured).

at_surface.description: Mirrors the IR relation.at_surface: WHERE on 'to' the gap was measured (A3/A4). 'realized' for curved/helical supports means solid-to-solid MinDistanceBetween, never a face label.

tol.description: Per-edge acceptance band half-width in 'units' (resolved from the IR relation.tol, or the document tolerance when absent). The §13 connectivity sweep applies it per the relation type's band (A3).

floating.description: Mirrors the floating opt-out (F): when the 'from' node is intended to float, this edge is informational and does NOT participate in the completeness clause (no UNCOVERED pressure).

penetration.description: Realized overlap depth in 'units' (target 0.5-2mm) for 'interpenetrate' edges feeding a boolean union (correction C3). Required for interpenetrate edges.

connectivity_entry.description: One realized connectivity measurement produced by the §13/C9 sweep for a single edge in a checkpoint's stage scope. The sweep is ONE execute_rhinoscript_python_code call per stage that loops the stage's declared edges, measures each realized solid-to-solid gap by GUID (A1/A4), and records the result here. Per B1 the in-Rhino sweep returns only VIOLATIONS (out_of_band + uncovered) as compact JSON; rhino-scene-state may persist passing entries too, but a stage is GREEN only when this list contains no out_of_band and no uncovered entry for any non-floating edge.

edge.description: The edge this measurement is for, identified by part_id (C1), echoing type/from/to (+to2 for spans_between).

gap.description: Realized signed gap in 'units' between the two LIVE solids (A1): positive = a real space between them; negative = interpenetration depth. Measured via Brep.ClosestPoint / Brep MinDistanceBetween read live by GUID from the document (NEVER against an IR-derived probe point, A1). For curved/helical supports it is the nearest-point-on-realized-solid distance (A4). Omitted when status='uncovered' (no measurement was possible).

status.description: pass = realized gap is inside this relation type's band (A3). out_of_band = measured but outside the band (e.g. a baluster 12mm short of the rail, or a column floating above the floor) => FAIL. uncovered = a declared contact with NO measurement, or a non-floating assembly part with NO declared+measured contact, or an edge pointing at a now-deleted part_id (C2) => FAIL. uncovered is THE clause that makes 'declared success with gaps' impossible (§13/C9 completeness).

measured_between.description: [guidA, guidB] of the two LIVE objects the gap was measured between (A1). Recording the GUIDs proves the measurement was solid-to-solid against the document, not against an IR coordinate. Omitted when status='uncovered' (one side has no live GUID).

band.description: [lo, hi] the per-relation-type acceptance band actually applied (A3), in 'units', for the report (e.g. on_top_of => [0, +tol]; interpenetrate => [-2,-0.5]). Informational.
